name: Abdul Rehman
role: Computer Science Student at NUST · Full Stack Developer · Founder of Stayza
headline: I build full stack products, end to end, and take them to production.
subtitles: Computer Science student at SEECS, NUST, passionate about building full stack applications from idea to deployment.
Founder of Stayza, a platform that helps students discover and connect with hostels.
Focused on software engineering, scalable systems, and creating practical solutions with real world impact.
shortBio: Computer Science student at NUST and founder of Stayza, a hostel discovery platform I built end to end. I care most about backend systems and shipping software that real people rely on.
about: I'm a Computer Science student at NUST, and the project that shaped how I think about engineering is Stayza, a hostel discovery platform I designed, built, and shipped to production on my own. The app itself matters less than what building it taught me: how systems actually fail, why simple structure beats clever structure, and what it takes to own something end to end.

I'm naturally curious about how things work under the hood. I'd rather understand why a system is designed a certain way, and how it could be made simpler or more reliable, than treat it as a black box. Most of what I know came from building real things rather than following tutorials, which means I've learned as much from debugging production issues at 2am as from any course.

Running Stayza meant more than writing code. I made product decisions, designed the user experience, tested releases, and shaped the platform around real feedback. That combination of backend architecture and product judgment is the kind of engineer I'm trying to become: someone who can take a vague problem and come back with something people actually use.

Right now I'm focused on growing Stayza, digging deeper into distributed systems and backend architecture, and becoming the kind of engineer who could build products used by millions, not just hundreds. Outside of code, I spend a lot of time reading about how well-built software is designed and picking apart products I like to figure out what makes them reliable at scale.
mission: To build scalable software, solve meaningful problems, and continuously grow as an engineer while creating technology that makes a lasting impact.
motto: Build with purpose. Learn continuously. Ship consistently.
currentFocus: Iterating on Stayza with real users
Learning system design and scalable backend architecture
